name: Pisco (General San Martín)
summary: Pisco is a sunny port town on Peru's desert Pacific coast, where giant sand dunes meet a cold ocean packed with wildlife. It is the gateway to the Ballestas Islands, often called the 'poor man's Galapagos'.
funFacts: The Ballestas Islands are nicknamed the 'Poor Man's Galapagos' because thousands of sea lions, penguins and seabirds crowd onto these tiny rocks just offshore.
On a hillside above the sea, somebody carved a giant 170-metre cactus-shaped picture called the Candelabra about 2,200 years ago, and nobody knows who or why.
The Nazca Lines are huge animal drawings, like a 93-metre hummingbird and a giant spider, scratched into the desert and only properly visible from a plane.
Real Humboldt penguins live here all year, named after a German explorer who studied the icy ocean current that brings them fish even in this hot desert.
The town's famous drink, pisco, is a clear brandy made from squashed grapes, and the word 'pisco' actually means 'bird' in the ancient Quechua language.
thingsToDo: Take a speedboat from Paracas harbour to the Ballestas Islands to see sea lions, penguins, pelicans and the giant Candelabra carved into a cliff.
Tour Paracas National Reserve to see Playa Roja's red sand, dramatic desert cliffs, the Cathedral rock viewpoint and pink flamingos in the bay.
Fly in a tiny plane over the Nazca Lines to look down at the giant hummingbird, monkey and spider drawings scratched into the desert.
Sandboard or ride a dune buggy down the giant sand dunes that surround the desert oasis of Huacachina.
Visit Tambo Colorado, a 500-year-old Inca palace whose mud-brick walls are still painted bright red, white and yellow.
Have lunch at El Chaco beach in Paracas with fresh ceviche while pelicans dive for fish nearby.

wildlife.name: Humboldt Penguin
wildlife.description: Black-and-white seabird that nests on the Ballestas rocks and swims in the chilly Humboldt current.

wildlife.name: South American Sea Lion
wildlife.description: Big barking sea mammal whose males grow a thick golden mane like a lion.

wildlife.name: Peruvian Pelican
wildlife.description: Huge brown seabird with a giant stretchy throat pouch for scooping up fish.

wildlife.name: Inca Tern
wildlife.description: Dark grey seabird with a bright red beak and a curly white moustache on each cheek.

localWord.meaning: Hello in Spanish, the language spoken in Pisco.

images.alt: Rocky Ballestas Islands rising out of the Pacific Ocean off the coast of Paracas, Peru.
The giant Candelabra geoglyph carved into a sandy hillside on the Paracas Peninsula.
The bright red sand of Playa Roja meeting blue ocean waves in Paracas National Reserve.
The desert oasis of Huacachina surrounded by giant sand dunes.
Aerial view of the famous Nazca Lines hummingbird drawn in the Peruvian desert.
Painted mud-brick walls of the Inca site Tambo Colorado in Peru's Pisco valley.
A Humboldt penguin standing on a rock.
A male South American sea lion with a thick golden mane resting on rocks.
A Peruvian pelican showing off its huge throat pouch.
An Inca tern with grey feathers, a red beak and a white moustache.
